data.title: As a DSO skill, I apply and remove tags via the first-class CLI instead of open-coding merge logic
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Refactor existing tag-write call sites to use the new `ticket tag`/`ticket untag` CLI instead of open-coded read-merge-write or full-replacement `ticket edit --tags=` patterns.

**Why**: The epic's premise is eliminating duplicated tag-merge logic and silent sibling-tag clobbering. Phase 2 adversarial review confirmed two additional full-replacement writers beyond the originally enumerated 4, both in the same pattern family and both sibling-destructive.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - Refactor `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` Phase 3 Step 3a — replace the read-merge-write block with direct `ticket tag <id> brainstorm:complete` + `ticket untag <id> scrutiny:pending` calls; each call site ≤3 lines
  - Refactor `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` Step 2.27 `interaction:deferred` writer and remover — both currently use `ticket edit --tags interaction:deferred` / `ticket edit --tags ""`, which REPLACE the entire tag list and silently destroy siblings (e.g., `CLI_user`, `design:approved`). Switch to `ticket tag <id> interaction:deferred` / `ticket untag <id> interaction:deferred`.
  - Refactor `plugins/dso/scripts/design-approve.sh` — replace the comma-joined list build with `ticket tag <id> design:approved`
  - Refactor `plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/prompts/ui-designer-dispatch-protocol.md` tag write steps to call the new CLI
  - Refactor `plugins/dso/skills/roadmap/SKILL.md` SCRUTINY_OPT_IN=false branch that writes `scrutiny:pending` via `ticket edit --tags="scrutiny:pending"` (full replacement) — switch to `ticket tag <epic-id> scrutiny:pending`
  - Update the canonical example in `plugins/dso/docs/contracts/interaction-deferred-tag.md` to reflect the new CLI
  - End-to-end smoke test for the brainstorm flow that places the PIL heading into an EDIT event (matching the production path, not a CREATE-description mock) and confirms `ticket tag brainstorm:complete` is accepted
- OUT:
  - Refactor of `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags.sh` internal tag writes — KEEP the direct-write path. The migration script uses explicit `author="ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags"` and `env_id="00000000-0000-4000-8000-migration001"` for migration provenance; routing it through `ticket tag` (which writes through ticket-edit.sh with the current session's author and env_id) would silently destroy the audit distinction between migration-era and normal-user tag events. Decision: adversarial-review finding F3 option (a) — scope the refactor to call sites outside the migration script and document this carve-out in Story 4's Tag Policy section.
  - New tag vocabulary; new event types

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a applies `brainstorm:complete` and removes `scrutiny:pending` via `.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag`/`untag`, with no inline merge code.
  ← Satisfies: "Brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a and design-approve.sh call the new commands directly instead of open-coding tag-merge logic."
- When this story is complete, brainstorm Step 2.27 writes and removes `interaction:deferred` via `.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag`/`untag`, and the canonical example in `interaction-deferred-tag.md` is updated to match.
  ← Satisfies: "Brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a and design-approve.sh call the new commands directly instead of open-coding tag-merge logic."
- When this story is complete, `design-approve.sh` sets `design:approved` via `.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag` instead of building a comma-joined list.
  ← Satisfies: "Brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a and design-approve.sh call the new commands directly instead of open-coding tag-merge logic."
- When this story is complete, `roadmap/SKILL.md` SCRUTINY_OPT_IN=false path writes `scrutiny:pending` via `.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag` instead of `ticket edit --tags=`.
  ← Satisfies: "Brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a and design-approve.sh call the new commands directly instead of open-coding tag-merge logic."
- When this story is complete, grep for `ticket edit --tags` outside the migration script and test fixtures returns zero hits across `plugins/dso/`.
  ← Satisfies: "Duplicated ~15-line read-merge-write blocks are deleted; new call sites are ≤3 lines each."
- When this story is complete, an end-to-end smoke test runs brainstorm on a fresh epic where the PIL heading is placed into an EDIT event (not a CREATE-description fixture) and confirms `brainstorm:complete` is applied without rejection.
  ← Satisfies: "A practitioner can complete a round-trip workflow — apply brainstorm:complete, later untag it to re-enter brainstorming, and re-apply it with PIL still present — without any lost tags, errors, or read-merge-write collisions."
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Migration script keeps its direct-write path. Preserves `author="ticket-migrate-brainstorm-tags"` and the dedicated migration `env_id`, which are load-bearing for any post-mortem audit of migration-era tag events. Decision from adversarial review finding F3, option (a).
- [Testing] The end-to-end smoke test MUST place the PIL via an EDIT event, not a CREATE description. A smoke that mocks PIL into CREATE description would mask the historical `_ticket_has_pil` gap on EDIT events and pass while real usage fails.
- [Maintainability] Open-coded full-replacement tag writes drop from 6 (4 original + `interaction:deferred` writer/remover + roadmap SCRUTINY_OPT_IN write) to 0 outside the migration script.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
